cheatsheet.title: Getting Started with Sandbox Hint Files

intro.description: This cheat sheet guides you through creating and using .sandbox-hint files — a lightweight DSL for defining automated code transformations that run as Eclipse Clean Up actions.

item.title: Understand the Concept
item.description:
A .sandbox-hint file contains one or more transformation rules. Each rule consists of:
• A source pattern that matches Java code (e.g. $x.getBytes("UTF-8"))
• An optional guard that restricts when the rule applies (e.g. sourceVersionGE(11))
• A replacement that rewrites the matched code (e.g. $x.getBytes(StandardCharsets.UTF_8))
Meta-variables like $x, $y, and $before$ capture parts of the source code and can be reused in the replacement.

item.title: Create Your First Hint File
item.description:
Use the New Sandbox Hint File wizard to create a hint file with metadata and an example rule.
Click Perform to open the wizard, then choose a project and folder, enter an ID and description, and select the Simple transformation rule template.

item.title: Write a Transformation Rule
item.description:
Open a .sandbox-hint file in the editor. A rule has the following structure:
source pattern :: guard
=> replacement
;;
Example:
$x.getBytes("UTF-8") :: sourceVersionGE(11)
=> $x.getBytes(java.nio.charset.StandardCharsets.UTF_8)
;;
The editor provides syntax highlighting, content assist (Ctrl+Space), and real-time validation.

item.title: Test Your Rule
item.description:
To apply your hint file rules to Java source code:
1. Open a Java file
2. Choose Source → Clean Up…
3. Select a custom profile and enable Sandbox TriggerPattern Hints
4. Click Finish
Matched code will be transformed according to your rules.

item.title: Create a Rule from Existing Code
item.description:
You can generate a rule directly from selected Java code:
1. Select a Java expression or statement in the editor
2. Press Ctrl+1 (Quick Assist)
3. Choose Generate DSL rule
The selected code is generalized into a source pattern with meta-variable placeholders.

item.title: Add Rules to a Plugin (Optional)
item.description:
To distribute your rules as a reusable Eclipse plugin:
1. Create a new Plug-in Project (File → New → Plug-in Project)
2. Open the plugin.xml editor
3. Add an extension for org.sandbox.jdt.triggerpattern.hints
4. Point the extension to your .sandbox-hint file or implement IHintProvider
This allows other developers to install your rules via the Eclipse Marketplace or a P2 update site.

item.title: Share Your Rules
item.description:
Hint files are plain text — you can share them by:
• Committing them to a shared Git repository
• Packaging them in a plugin and publishing to an update site
• Exporting via File → Export → Deployable plug-ins
Congratulations! You now know how to create, test, and share Sandbox Hint Files.
